title: Kattil Madam Temple, Chalapuram
tagline: Kattil Madam Temple, Chalapuram — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Kattil Madam Temple is a dilapidated shrine in the Palakkad district in Kerala, India.
overview.description: Kattil Madam Temple is a dilapidated shrine in the Palakkad district in Kerala, India. It is thought to be a Jain temple built around the ninth or tenth centuries AD and is situated on the Pattambi Guruvayur road.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Vavanoor
topPlaces.description: Vavanoor is a village in Pattambi taluk in Palakkad district of Kerala state, south India. The village is located near Koottanad, Pattambi.

topPlaces.name: Nagalassery
topPlaces.description: Nagalassery is a village and gram panchayat in Palakkad district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Muthuthala
topPlaces.description: Muthuthala is a village and gram panchayat. It lies northwest of Pattambi in Palakkad district, of the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Mezhathur
topPlaces.description: Mezhathur is an Indian village in the state of Kerala situated at Pattambi taluk of Palakkad district at the border of Thrissur, Malappuram and Palakkad districts. It is a historical and cultural village in Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Thirumittakode Anchumoorthi Temple
topPlaces.description: Thirumittakode Anchumoorthi Temple (also called Thiruviththuvakkōdu) is a Hindu temple equally associated with the deities Vishnu and Shiva. Temple is situated on the banks of the Bharathappuzha at Thirumittacode of Palakkad District in Kerala state in India.

topPlaces.name: Koottanad
topPlaces.description: Koottanad is a town in Nagalassery Panchayath of Pattambi taluk of Palakkad district, in the state of Kerala. It is situated at the border of Thrissur, Palakkad and Malapuram districts.

topPlaces.name: Karukaputhur
topPlaces.description: Karukaputhur also spell as Karukaputhoor, is a place on the Pattambi taluk, Palakkad district of Kerala. This area part of the Thrithala legislative assembly and Thirumittacode Panchayath.

topPlaces.name: Trithala Maha Siva Temple
topPlaces.description: Trithala Maha Siva Temple (Malayalam: തൃത്താല മഹാ ശിവക്ഷേത്രം) is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Shiva is situated on the banks of the Bharathappuzha river at Thrithala of Palakkad District in Kerala state in India. References to this temple are found in Aithihyamala of Kottarathil Sankunni and many classics of Malayalam Literature.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kattil Madam Temple, Chalapuram?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kattil Madam Temple, Chalapuram is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kattil Madam Temple, Chalapuram?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Calicut International Airport, Kozhikode (46 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (90 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kattil Madam Temple, Chalapuram?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kattil Madam Temple, Chalapuram at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kattil Madam Temple, Chalapuram?
faq.a: Stays in Kattil Madam Temple, Chalapuram start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kattil Madam Temple, Chalapuram?
faq.a: The top attractions are Vavanoor, Nagalassery, Muthuthala, Mezhathur, Thirumittakode Anchumoorthi Temple.
